question.text: The call taxi industry in India going through all business life cycle phases including decline due to app-based aggregators is an example of:

answer.text: A cyclical trend that will reverse
A secular trend driven by technology disruption causing business life cycle displacement
A regulatory change by the government

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Call taxis declined due to technology disruption (app-based taxi aggregators like Ola, Uber). This is a secular trend causing displacement — a new technology replaced an existing business model. **The Trap:** It is not cyclical (won't reverse), seasonal, or primarily regulatory. It is a technology-driven secular disruption. **Cross-Exam Context:** Technology-driven secular trends tested in NISM Series XV Chapter 6. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When analyzing an industry, always ask: Is there an emerging technology that could disrupt this business model? If yes, what is the timeline?
